name: Corned Beef Brisket with Stir-Fried Cabbage
recipeIngredient: 8 cups Sliced cabbage, cut 1/2 inch thick
1 medium Red bell pepper, cut into thin strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Place corned beef in a Dutch oven and add water to cover. Cover tightly and simmer for 3 to 4 hours or until the meat is tender.
2. Shortly before serving, heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add cabbage and red pepper, stir-fry for 5 minutes.
3. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and continue cooking cabbage and red pepper for an additional 5 minutes.
4. Stir in sugar, vinegar, caraway seeds, and salt into the cabbage mixture, then continue stirring for 2 more minutes.
5. Place brisket, fat side up, on a broiler-safe serving platter with the surface of the meat 3 to 4 inches from the heat. Brush melted jelly over the brisket.
6. Broil for 5 minutes or until the brisket is glazed. Carve the brisket diagonally across the grain into thin slices and serve with cabbage.
7. Makes 10 servings. If desired, potatoes and carrots can be added to the corned beef during the last half-hour of simmering. Reserve the braising liquid for making split pea soup later. Strain and chill the cooled broth, removing any fat that hardens at the surface before using the defatted stock in the soup.
